##NAMES=Fluor-rewitzerite
##RRUFFID=R250089
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[(H_2_O)K]Mn_2_(Al_2_Ti)(PO_4_)_4_(OF)(H_2_O)_10_·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Hagendorf-Süd pegmatite, Oberpfalz, Bavaria, Germany
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Clusters of colourless stubby prisms on the walls of vugs in corroded zwieselite, in association with Zn- and Al-bearing earlshannonite, fluorapatite, jahnsite-(CaMnMn) and Al-rich strunzite. Described in Hochleitner et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250089
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=K_1.15_Mn_1.50_Mg_0.09_Fe^2+^_0.41_Fe^3+^_0.42_Al_1.70_Ti^4+^_0.89_P_3.99_F_0.92_O_32.02_H_29.94_ ; Normalised to 9(M1 + M2 + M3 + P) apfu
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.425 b: 20.591 c: 12.285 alpha: 90 beta: 90.47 gamma: 90 volume: 2637 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
